channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Islamic State-inspired driver expressed desire to kill before deadly New Orleans rampage, Biden says
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A U.S. Army veteran driving a pickup truck that bore the flag of the Islamic State group wrought carnage on New Orleans’ raucous New Year’s celebration, killing 15 people as he steered around a police blockade and slammed into revelers before being shot dead by police.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, JIM MUSTIAN, KEVIN McGILL and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Latest: Death toll in New Orleans crash rises to 15, coroner says
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A driver behind the wheel of a pickup truck rammed into a crowd of New Orleans revelers on Bourbon Street early on New Year’s Day, killing at least 15 people in what the FBI is investigating as an act of terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What we know about a vehicle attack on pedestrians in New Orleans that killed at least 15
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Authorities say the driver of a pickup truck sped through a crowd of pedestrians gathered in New Orleans’ bustling French Quarter district early on New Year's Day, killing at least 15 people and injuring about 30 other revelers. The suspect was killed in a shootout with police.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jocelyne Wildenstein, Swiss socialite known for extreme plastic surgeries, dies at 84: reports
item.description: Wildenstein's facial surgeries were notably feline, leading the press to (cruelly) dub her "Catwoman.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, Fox 5

item.title: Pro-ISIS group called on Muslims to conduct NYE attacks ahead of New Orleans massacre
item.description: A pro-ISIS group called on Muslims to conduct attacks in the US, Russia and Europe just days before a man drove a truck into a crowd of people in New Orleans on New Year's Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FBI says it thwarted apparent plot against AIPAC
item.description: Law enforcement arrested Forrest Kendall Pemberton after he allegedly traveled to Plantation, Fla., in search of AIPAC’s office with several firearms, including an AR-15 rifle.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Orleans barriers to prevent vehicle attacks were in the process of being replaced
item.description: Seven years ago, New Orleans officials began installing adjustable barriers at intersections in the famed French Quarter to temporarily prevent vehicles from entering the tourist area where the narrow streets are typically teeming with pedestrians every night of the week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 1 dead, 7 injured in Tesla fire, explosion at Las Vegas Trump International Hotel; sources say it wasn't an accident
item.description: A car fire at the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas caused smoke and loud booms, disrupting guests and nearby residents.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, WGN 9

item.title: 1 dead, 7 injured after Tesla Cybertruck explosion outside Trump hotel in Las Vegas: Police
item.description: Police in Las Vegas responded to Trump Tower on Wednesday morning for reports of an explosion near the front valet entrance to the hotel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Orleans terror attack in photos
item.description: A driver wrought carnage on New Orleans' famed French Quarter early on New Year's Day, killing at least 10 people as he steered around a police blockade and rammed a pickup truck into a crowd before being shot to death by police, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Power is restored to nearly all of Puerto Rico after a major blackout
item.description: Electric power has been restored to nearly all affected customers across Puerto Rico after a sweeping blackout plunged the U.S. territory into darkness on New Year’s Eve.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Pérez Pintado, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sugar Bowl postponed to Thursday night
item.description: Hours following the New Year's Day tragedy on Bourbon Street, Wednesday's Allstate Sugar Bowl bout between Notre Dame and Georgia is being postponed.
item.dc:creator: Richie Mills, KRON4

item.title: 1 person dies when Tesla Cybertruck catches fire and explodes outside Trump's Las Vegas hotel
item.description: Authorities say one person died and seven others people were injured when a Tesla Cybertruck caught fire and exploded outside President-elect Donald Trump’s Las Vegas hotel.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: FBI finds 150 homemade bombs at Virginia home in one of the largest such seizures, prosecutors say
item.description: The FBI said it believes it's the largest amount of homemade explosives it has encountered in its history
item.dc:creator: Sean C. Davis, ABC 4

item.title: The world welcomes 2025 with light shows, embraces and ice plunges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — From Sydney to Mumbai to Paris to Rio de Janeiro, communities around the world welcomed 2025 with spectacular light shows, embraces and ice plunges.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, CEDAR ATTANASIO and CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Monkey discovered during California traffic stop is getting a new home
item.description: MADERA COUNTY, Calif. (KSEE/KGPE) - A monkey discovered during a bizarre traffic stop in Madera County, California, on Tuesday is getting a new home. Officials pulled over a 2022 Rolls Royce Ghost at around 8 p.m. for speeding on Highway 99, north of Avenue 17.
item.dc:creator: Briana Byus, NewsNation

item.title: New York employers must now offer paid medical leave during pregnancy
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — Pregnant New Yorkers will be entitled to at least 20 hours of paid leave to attend prenatal medical appointments under a law that took effect Wednesday. Gov. Kathy Hochul said the policy makes New York the first state in the country to offer paid leave for prenatal care.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Orleans New Year's Day ramming is the latest attack to use a vehicle as a deadly weapon
item.description: Authorities say a man who drove a pickup truck at high speed into a crowd of people celebrating the new year in New Orleans was hell-bent on creating carnage.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: L.A. County officials voting on measure to honor late former President Jimmy Carter
item.description: Los Angeles County officials are set to vote on a motion to honor former President Jimmy Carter, who died Sunday at the age of 100. L.A. County Board of Supervisors Chair Kathryn Barger and Supervisor Hilda Solis have introduced the motion to declare Thursday, Jan. 9, a countywide day of mourning.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Watch a replay of the 2025 Rose Parade presented by Honda
item.description: LA's Very Own KTLA 5 is proud to offer live, nonstop coverage of the 136th Rose Parade presented by Honda on New Year’s Day, Jan. 1, 2025. Our broadcast and streaming coverage begins at 4 a.m. with an encore presentation of the 2024 Tournament of Roses Parade with grand marshal Audra McDonald.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, WFLA, NBC

item.title: German chancellor takes veiled swipe at Elon Musk in New Year's address
item.description: Scholz's comments come days after the German government accused the billionaire of trying to influence its February election.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, NewsNation

item.title: Hundreds apply for restitution for abuse suffered at Florida reform schools
item.description: Hundreds of people who say they suffered physical or sexual abuse at two state-run reform schools in Florida are in line to receive tens of thousands of dollars in restitution from the state, after Florida lawmakers formally apologized for the horrors they endured as children more than 50 years ago.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Human remains found near Electric Forest music festival site
item.description: Human remains have been discovered not far from where an Oakland County man vanished during the Electric Forest music festival in 2018.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Van Gilder, NewsNation

item.title: New California law takes aim at homework burden on students
item.description: You may remember spending hours after school doing homework. A lawmaker in California is hope to change that for the next generation of students in the state.
item.dc:creator: Eytan Wallace, KRON4

item.title: Neighbors' dispute escalates when Florida man allegedly slashes woman with knife
item.description: Body camera footage shared by a Florida sheriff's office shows a stabbing suspect surrendering to a deputy after an alleged dispute with a neighbor.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police investigate stolen license plate, find drugs and stolen meat
item.description: Mt. Juliet officers discovered a 48-year-old Nashville woman who reportedly confessed to stealing meat from area stores over multiple dates.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Barker, NewsNation

item.title: Nearly all of Puerto Rico is without power on New Year's Eve
item.description: A blackout hit nearly all of Puerto Rico early Tuesday as the U.S. territory prepared to celebrate New Year’s, leaving more than 1.3 million clients in the dark. Officials said it could take up to two days to restore power.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: American family shot on Mexico vacation in attack that left 3 dead, teen fighting for life: reports
item.description: A Chicago teenager vacationing in Mexico is in a coma after he, his father and his uncle became targets of a shooting last week. The father and uncle were killed in the attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australia, New Zealand first to welcome 2025 with fireworks
item.description: The U.S. countdown is still hours away, but those in New Zealand have already started 2025 with a bang.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Have any New Year's resolutions? Here are some tips to help make them stick
item.description: Studies have shown that up to 70% of people who make New Year’s resolutions abandon those good intentions within months. Here are some tips for how to keep those resolutions when your willpower starts to falter.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Oregon men search for Sasquatch, die in Washington forest
item.description: Two men from Portland searching for Sasquatch and missing since early Christmas morning were found dead in the Gifford Pinchot National Forest, likely from exposure and being ill-prepared.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, ABC 4

item.title: Democrats taunt Trump with 'President Musk' moniker
item.description: Democrats are taunting President-elect Trump with claims that billionaire entrepreneur Elon Musk is calling the shots instead of the incoming president.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: Winning ticket for $1.22 billion lottery jackpot sold in California, Mega Millions says
item.description: At least one Mega Millions player has plenty of dough to ring in the New Year after drawing the winning number.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
